title: allium subangulatum
intro: Allium subangulatum is a type of plant that belongs to the Allium genus, commonly known as wild garlic or onions. It is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia, characterized by its narrow leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Allium subangulatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
